title: RAGFlow - Open-source RAG engine with Agent capabilities
snippet: Leading open-source Retrieval-Augmented Generation engine that fuses cutting-edge RAG with Agent capabilities to create a superior context layer for LLMs. 76.6K stars.

metadata.llm_reason: Top open-source RAG engine with massive adoption, directly relevant to RAG frameworks survey

title: Microsoft GraphRAG - Graph-based RAG system
snippet: A modular graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system. 31.8K stars. Uses knowledge graphs with community summaries for enhanced retrieval.

metadata.llm_reason: Major graph-based RAG framework from Microsoft Research, key innovation in the field

title: LightRAG - Simple and Fast RAG (EMNLP 2025)
snippet: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation. Graph-based approach building knowledge graphs from documents for connected, contextual retrieval. 31.2K stars.

metadata.llm_reason: Academic RAG framework with massive traction, EMNLP 2025 paper, graph-based approach

title: RAG Techniques - Advanced techniques collection
snippet: Showcases various advanced techniques for RAG systems combining information retrieval with generative models. 26.3K stars. Comprehensive reference.

metadata.llm_reason: Comprehensive collection of RAG techniques with high community adoption, excellent reference

title: Haystack - AI orchestration framework for RAG and agents
snippet: Open-source AI orchestration framework for building context-engineered, production-ready LLM applications. Modular pipelines and agent workflows. 24.6K stars.

metadata.llm_reason: Major open-source RAG orchestration framework, production-ready with modular design

title: RAG-Anything - All-in-One RAG Framework
snippet: All-in-One RAG Framework from HKUDS. 14.7K stars. Handles multiple document types and modalities.

metadata.llm_reason: Highly starred new RAG framework supporting multi-modal document processing

title: R2R - Production-ready Agentic RAG with RESTful API
snippet: SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API. 7.7K stars.

metadata.llm_reason: Production-focused agentic RAG system with REST API, represents the agentic RAG trend

title: Verba - RAG chatbot powered by Weaviate
snippet: Retrieval Augmented Generation chatbot powered by Weaviate vector database. 7.6K stars.

metadata.llm_reason: Demonstrates vector DB integration with RAG, from major vector database vendor

title: AutoRAG - RAG Evaluation and Optimization with AutoML
snippet: Open-Source Framework for RAG Evaluation and Optimization with AutoML-Style Automation. 4.6K stars.

metadata.llm_reason: Unique AutoML approach to RAG optimization - evaluates and selects best pipeline configurations

title: Cognita - Modular open source RAG framework by TrueFoundry
snippet: RAG Framework for building modular, open source applications for production. 4.3K stars.

metadata.llm_reason: Enterprise-focused modular RAG framework with production deployment features

title: OpenRAG - RAG platform built on Langflow, Docling, Opensearch
snippet: Comprehensive, single package RAG platform built on Langflow, Docling, and Opensearch. 3.6K stars.

metadata.llm_reason: New integrated RAG platform combining multiple open-source tools, launched 2025

title: HippoRAG - RAG inspired by human long-term memory (NeurIPS 2024)
snippet: Novel RAG framework inspired by human long-term memory enabling LLMs to continuously integrate knowledge. RAG + Knowledge Graphs + Personalized PageRank. NeurIPS 2024.

metadata.llm_reason: Novel neuroscience-inspired RAG approach from top venue (NeurIPS 2024), knowledge graph integration

title: ragapp - Easiest way to use Agentic RAG in enterprise
snippet: The easiest way to use Agentic RAG in any enterprise. 4.4K stars. Built on LlamaIndex.

metadata.llm_reason: Enterprise-focused agentic RAG with simplified deployment, represents market trend

title: A-RAG - Agentic RAG with Hierarchical Retrieval Interfaces
snippet: State-of-the-art RAG framework with keyword, semantic, and chunk read tools for multi-hop QA. Hierarchical retrieval interfaces.

metadata.llm_reason: Implements cutting-edge A-RAG paper with hierarchical retrieval, very recent (Feb 2026)

title: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey on Agentic RAG
snippet: Survey exploring Agentic RAG which transcends traditional RAG by embedding autonomous AI agents into the RAG pipeline with reflection, planning, tool use, and multiagent collaboration.

metadata.llm_reason: Comprehensive survey of the agentic RAG paradigm, key reference for understanding the field direction

title: A-RAG: Scaling Agentic RAG via Hierarchical Retrieval Interfaces
snippet: Scaling Agentic RAG via Hierarchical Retrieval Interfaces. Provides keyword_search, semantic_search, and chunk_read tools enabling adaptive multi-granularity retrieval.

metadata.llm_reason: State-of-the-art agentic RAG paper from Feb 2026, introduces hierarchical retrieval interface design

title: A Comprehensive Survey of RAG: Evolution, Current Landscape and Future Directions
snippet: Comprehensive survey covering RAG evolution from naive to advanced to modular approaches, current landscape, and future research directions.

metadata.llm_reason: Major comprehensive RAG survey covering the full evolution of the field

title: A Survey of Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Customized LLMs
snippet: GraphRAG survey addressing traditional RAG limitations through graph-structured knowledge representation and multihop reasoning.

metadata.llm_reason: Focused survey on Graph RAG approaches, covers Microsoft GraphRAG and alternatives

title: Reconstructing Context: Evaluating Advanced Chunking Strategies for RAG
snippet: Evaluates advanced chunking strategies including semantic, late, and contextual chunking for retrieval-augmented generation systems.

metadata.llm_reason: Rigorous evaluation of chunking strategies - key engineering decision in RAG pipeline design

title: 15 Best Open-Source RAG Frameworks in 2026
snippet: Comprehensive comparison of open-source RAG frameworks including RAGFlow, LangChain, LlamaIndex, Haystack, and newer entrants. Covers features, architecture, and use cases.

metadata.llm_reason: Recent comparison article covering the current RAG framework landscape with practical guidance

title: From RAG to Context - A 2025 year-end review of RAG
snippet: Year-end review discussing RAG evolution toward Context Engineering. RAG vs long context debate, emergence of agentic RAG, and shift toward intelligent context assembly.

metadata.llm_reason: Authoritative year-end review from RAGFlow team covering RAG's evolution and future direction

title: RAG vs. long-context LLMs: A side-by-side comparison
snippet: Side-by-side comparison of RAG and long-context LLMs covering accuracy, cost, latency, and use cases. Concludes they are complementary, not competing.

metadata.llm_reason: Clear comparison of RAG vs long context trade-offs with practical guidance

title: Long Context vs. RAG: Strategies for Processing Long Documents (SIGIR 2025)
snippet: Academic study from SIGIR 2025 comparing long context and RAG strategies for processing long documents in LLMs.

metadata.llm_reason: Peer-reviewed academic comparison from top IR venue, provides empirical evidence

title: Long Context RAG Performance of LLMs - Databricks Blog
snippet: Databricks analysis of how RAG performance changes with long context LLMs, including benchmarks and practical recommendations.

metadata.llm_reason: Industry benchmark from Databricks on RAG performance with long context, practical insights

title: From Local to Global: A Graph RAG Approach to Query-Focused Summarization
snippet: Original Microsoft GraphRAG paper. Graph-based approach to RAG that builds knowledge graphs with community summaries for global question answering.

metadata.llm_reason: Foundational GraphRAG paper from Microsoft Research, introduced the graph-based RAG paradigm

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks (Lewis et al., 2020)
snippet: Original RAG paper combining retrieval with seq2seq generation. Foundational work that launched the entire RAG field.

metadata.llm_reason: The foundational RAG paper - essential for any comprehensive coverage of the field

title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique (Asai et al., ICLR 2024)
snippet: Self-reflective RAG that trains an LM to adaptively retrieve, generate, and critique its own output using reflection tokens.

metadata.llm_reason: Key innovation in adaptive RAG - model decides when to retrieve and self-critiques output

title: RAPTOR: Recursive Abstractive Processing for Tree-Organized Retrieval
snippet: Builds hierarchical tree of document summaries at multiple abstraction levels, enabling retrieval at different granularities. ICLR 2024.

metadata.llm_reason: Novel hierarchical retrieval approach, addresses the granularity problem in RAG

title: ColBERT: Efficient and Effective Passage Search via Late Interaction (Khattab & Zaharia, 2020)
snippet: Late interaction retrieval model using token-level similarity matching. Enables efficient yet effective neural retrieval. Foundation for DSPy creator's work.

metadata.llm_reason: Foundational dense retrieval technique, important for understanding modern retrieval methods

title: HyDE: Hypothetical Document Embeddings (Gao et al., 2022)
snippet: Generate a hypothetical answer document first, then use it for retrieval. Zero-shot dense retrieval without relevance labels.

metadata.llm_reason: Innovative query transformation technique widely used in RAG pipelines
